Long time Fortran programmer here. 

I am totally new to lfortran and wasm but very interested on this project since it seems that everything is a web-app, these days.

So, I followed the installation instructions and attempting to compile and run the "matmul2d" example, but I am getting the following error in the browser after opening more tools --> development tools: 

```
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (File not found)    matmul2d.js
Uncaught ReferenceError: Module is not defined                                         index     
    at allocArrOnHeap ((index):59:17)
    at calculate ((index):129:27)
    at HTMLButtonElement.onclick ((index):28:80)
```
Any hints? 

Please ask for whatever info you may need.

If I take 
```
    index.html
    matmul2d.js
    matmul2d.wasm 
```
from the repo, the screen shows the correct graphics; but, 
the calculate button does not produce any output and, instead,
I get the following error: 
```
    Uncaught TypeError: Module._malloc is not a function
        at allocArrOnHeap ((index):66:35)
        at calculate ((index):129:27)
        at HTMLButtonElement.onclick ((index):28:80)
```

So, that's before I even attempt to re-build the fortran source.

I tried the pre-compiled files from the repo: 
 - in Linux/Chrome and I get the `_malloc is not a function` error
 - in Windows/Chrome and I get the `_malloc is not a function` error
 - in Windows/Edge and they work
 - in Linux/Edge and they work
 - in Linux/Firefox and they work

I am totally new to lfortran and wasm but very interested on this project since it seems that everything is a web-app, these days.

So, I followed the installation instructions and attempting to compile and run the "matmul2d" example, but I am getting the following error in the browser after opening more tools --> development tools:

Any hints? 

Please ask for whatever info you may need.

```
LFORTRAN_CC=emcc
EMSDK_NODE=/home/<userid>/local/programs/emsdk/node/20.18.0_64bit/bin/node
EMSDK_PATH=/home/<userid>/local/programs/emsdk
PATH=/home/<userid>/local/programs/emsdk:/home/<userid>/local/programs/emsdk/upstream/emscripten:...
```

If I do 
`lfortran matmul2d.f90  --backend=llvm  --target=wasm32-unknown-emscripten`
I get a *.wasm file and *.out file, where the latter is actually a javascript file; so, I don't know how to run that

If I do 
`lfortran matmul2d.f90  --backend=llvm  --target=wasm32-unknown-emscripten  --wasm-html`
I do get one of each *.html, *.js, *.wasm

If I run the python http server and go to port 8000 in Edge or Firefox, I get the screenshot posted above and the following at the command line: 

```
python -m http.server 
Serving HTTP on 0.0.0.0 port 8000 (http://0.0.0.0:8000/) ...
127.0.0.1 - - [22/Nov/2024 21:52:18] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 304 -
127.0.0.1 - - [22/Nov/2024 21:52:18] "GET /matmul2d.out.js HTTP/1.1" 304 -
127.0.0.1 - - [22/Nov/2024 21:52:18] "GET /matmul2d.out.wasm HTTP/1.1" 304 -
127.0.0.1 - - [22/Nov/2024 21:52:18] code 404, message File not found
127.0.0.1 - - [22/Nov/2024 21:52:18] "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.1" 404 -
```

Long time Fortran programmer here. 

I am totally new to lfortran and wasm but very interested on this project since it seems that everything is a web-app, these days.

So, I followed the installation instructions and attempting to compile and run the "matmul2d" example, but I am getting the following error in the browser after opening more tools --> development tools: 

```
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (File not found)    matmul2d.js
Uncaught ReferenceError: Module is not defined                                         index     
    at allocArrOnHeap ((index):59:17)
    at calculate ((index):129:27)
    at HTMLButtonElement.onclick ((index):28:80)
```
Any hints? 

Please ask for whatever info you may need.

Thank you very much for that; at least I am now sure that my setup is working.

Here is my use case. 

There is a good old gui-based desktop app, with many many widgets, some to collect input, some to show/visualize output; as part of its functionality, this app makes system calls to run program executables produced from various C and Fortran programs. The app and the various C/Fortran programs are developed/maintained/built in a native manner in both Windows and Linux, and the resulting files finally deployed to their corresponding platforms. 

Thus, of course, the idea would be to (re-)write the desktop app using a web development framework and compile the various C/Fortran programs to wasm; then, the development/maintenance needs to be done just once and in one platform (either one).

What would the recommended approach be? 
 - Continue to have C/Fortran sources as full programs, or
 - Turn the C/Fortran sources into callable functions
 - any other choice?

Correct me if I am wrong, but I presume that all I would need from lfortran is the generated *.wasm files; then, separately, a way to call these *.wasm program/functions from javascript needs to be implemented.

So, which way to go and how?
